scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (A, C#, E) plus safe extensions (B, F#). No avoid notes. The pentatonic structure guarantees instant consonance over the I major chord.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (A) and major 3rd (C#) for strong resolution. The F# (13th) adds brightness. Start and end phrases on chord tones for strongest effect.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for I chord vamps and major tonality establishment. Ideal for melodic hooks, solos, and improvisation. Works universally across rock, country, blues, and pop.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The source scale for A major harmony. Contains all chord tones and available tensions. Provides complete melodic freedom while maintaining strong tonal center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. Emphasize chord tones on strong beats, tensions on weak beats. The D (11th) can sound suspended, so resolve it to C# or E.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete melodic freedom over I major chord. Essential for compositional development, classical approaches, and full diatonic expression in A major.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds D# (#11) for dreamy, floating quality over major chord. The raised 4th creates an ethereal, modern sound perfect for film scores and progressive music.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (D#) for characteristic Lydian sound. Creates bright, uplifting quality. Works beautifully for sustained I major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use over Amaj7 or Amaj7#11 for modern, ethereal sound. Perfect for film music, jazz fusion, progressive rock, and dream pop. Creates floating, optimistic mood.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Converts major triad to dominant 7th character with G natural. Perfect for bluesy, rock-oriented approaches to the I chord. Common in jam bands and folk rock.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The G natural (b7) creates bluesy edge. Great for rock and blues guitar. Emphasize the C# and G for dominant character.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for A7 chords and dominant vamps. Essential for rock, blues, country, and jam band styles. Use when you want major sound with bluesy edge.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Built on the V - creates bluesy major sound
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes are diatonic to A major, creating safe bluesy sound. The minor pentatonic from the V degree works perfectly over I major for rock and country styles.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Creates bluesy major sound without clash. The G natural adds slight tension. Perfect for rock guitar soloing over major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential rock guitar scale over A major. Used extensively from Clapton to Mayer. Safe and consonant but with blues character. Perfect for major key rock solos.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Parent scale - perfect alignment with maj7 chord
scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones plus available tensions. The G# is a chord tone (maj7), making Ionian the natural choice for Imaj7 chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (G#) for sophisticated sound. All scale tones work beautifully. The D (11th) can be used but resolve to C# or E for clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Imaj7 chords. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B, and contemporary music. The quintessential maj7 chord scale.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The raised 4th (D#) creates a modern, dreamy maj7 sound. This is the preferred mode for Imaj7 in jazz and fusion contexts.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (D#) is the signature sound. Creates floating, ethereal quality. Perfect for modal vamps on Imaj7 chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Amaj7#11 or modern Imaj7 sounds. Essential for jazz, fusion, film scores, and progressive rock. The go-to scale for sophisticated maj7 harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Omits the 4th and 7th, focusing on the core consonant tones. Simpler approach to maj7 harmony, perfect for melodic clarity.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Simple and melodic approach. The lack of G# means you're implying the maj7 rather than stating it. Great for hooks and memorable melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for pop and R&B approaches to maj7 chords. Creates smooth, singable melodies without harmonic complexity. Ideal for vocal lines.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Primary ii chord scale in A major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Dorian is the natural ii mode in A major (shares all notes with A major). The major 6th (G#) distinguishes it from natural minor, creating a brighter, jazzier sound perfect for ii chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the major 6th (G#) for characteristic Dorian sound. This is essential for ii-V-I progressions. The C# (9th) adds sophistication.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for ii chord in A major. Perfect for Bm7-E7-Amaj7 progressions. The standard choice for jazz, pop, R&B, and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural minor with G natural instead of G#. Creates darker, more somber quality. Traditional minor sound, more melancholic than Dorian.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The G natural (b13) creates darker color. Use when you want more traditional, sad minor sound over ii chord. Common in rock and classical.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. Perfect for melancholic sections. More traditional than Dorian. Common in rock ballads and emotional contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Safe minor pentatonic removes complexity. Contains core minor tones without the characteristic 6th. Universal minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Simple and direct. Great for straightforward minor melodies. The A (b7) adds slight tension. Perfect for beginners.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, bluesy approach to ii minor. Works in rock, blues, and pop contexts. Safe choice for improvisation.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Dorian contains all four chord tones and provides the perfect color for iim7. The major 6th (G#) creates the bright, jazzy character essential for ii-V-I progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The G# (13th) is crucial for Dorian color. Creates smooth voice leading to E7 (V7). This is the standard ii-V-I sound in jazz and contemporary music.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for Bm7-E7-Amaj7. The definitive ii minor 7 scale in jazz, fusion, pop, R&B, and neo-soul. Use for sophisticated minor harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Thinking in the parent A major scale over Bm7 reinforces key center. All notes work perfectly since Bm7 is diatonic to A major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major but emphasize B as the tonal center. Creates strong connection to the key. Perfect for maintaining tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use when you want to emphasize the key center relationship. Great for teaching and understanding diatonic harmony. Works in all contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes harmonic complexity while maintaining minor character. Safe for simple melodic lines and beginner improvisation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and simple. Won't clash but lacks the sophistication of Dorian. Good for straightforward minor melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple pop and rock approaches. Great for beginners. Use when you want clear, uncomplicated minor sound.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii chord mode in A major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: C# Phrygian is the iii mode of A major. The characteristic b2 (D natural) creates exotic, Spanish-influenced color. Less common than ii or vi but effective for drama.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (D) for characteristic Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality. The b9 adds tension and exotic flavor.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use over iii minor for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced sounds. Creates tension and drama in A major. Common in progressive rock and flamenco.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Provides more traditional minor sound than Phrygian. The natural 2nd (D#) is brighter than Phrygian's b2. More conventional minor color.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More straightforward than Phrygian. The D# (9th) creates smoother melodic motion. Less exotic, more conventional.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use when you want traditional minor sound on iii chord. Less dramatic than Phrygian. Works for straightforward minor passages.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes of A major work over C#m since it's diatonic. Maintains strong connection to key center. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major while emphasizing C# as root. Maintains tonal clarity. The D natural creates Phrygian flavor automatically.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center while playing over iii. Simple approach that works universally. Great for diatonic thinking.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: The iii chord's natural mode in major keys. The b2 creates Spanish/exotic character. All four chord tones are present with characteristic Phrygian color.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The b9 (D) creates strong characteristic sound. Use for dark, dramatic, or Spanish-influenced passages. Creates tension within major key context.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for exotic minor color in A major. Use in progressive rock, metal, Spanish music, and dramatic contexts. Less common but highly effective.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple approach using the parent major scale. All notes work since iiim7 is fully diatonic. Maintains clear key center relationship.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize C# as root. Simple and effective. The D creates Phrygian flavor automatically.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for straightforward diatonic approach. Perfect for maintaining key center awareness. Works in all styles and contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D major is the natural scale for the IV chord. Creates strong subdominant function. Essential for I-IV-V progressions and folk/rock harmony.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the root (D) and major 3rd (F#). The G (11th) can sound suspended, resolve to F# or A. Perfect for folk and rock IV chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Perfect for folk, rock, country, and pop. The subdominant function is fundamental to Western harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (G#) creates modern, floating IV chord sound. Common in film music and jazz. Avoids the suspended sound of the natural 11th.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (G#) creates bright, modern sound. Perfect for Dmaj7#11. Very common in jazz and contemporary music over IV chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV chord sound. Perfect for jazz, fusion, R&B, and film music. Creates dreamy subdominant quality.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes the 4th and 7th for guaranteed consonance. Perfect for country, folk, and rock styles. Simple and instantly singable.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - all notes are perfectly consonant. Great for melodies and hooks. The lack of G avoids suspended sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for country, folk, and rock IV chords. Ideal for memorable melodies and vocal lines. Essential for singer-songwriter styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Using the A major scale over IV chord maintains key center. All notes work since D is the IV of A. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize D as root. Maintains tonal clarity. All diatonic tones work perfectly.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for diatonic approach to IV chord. Maintains key center relationship. Works universally in all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lydian is the standard choice for IVmaj7 in jazz and contemporary music. The #11 (G#) avoids the suspended sound of the natural 11th and creates bright, modern character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (G#) is the signature sound. Creates floating, dreamy quality. This is the definitive IVmaj7 sound in jazz and fusion.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for modern IVmaj7 harmony. Perfect for jazz, fusion, R&B, and contemporary pop. Use for Dmaj7#11 specifically. Creates sophisticated subdominant color.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D Ionian provides all chord tones. The natural 11th (G) can sound suspended but works in traditional contexts. More conventional than Lydian.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use with care - the natural 11th (G) can clash. Resolve G to F# or A for smoothness. Better for traditional contexts.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for traditional IVmaj7 sound. Works in folk, country, and classical contexts. More conventional than Lydian approach.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major scale contains all IVmaj7 tones. Simple diatonic thinking. Maintains strong key center relationship.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize D as root. Notice the G# creates natural Lydian flavor. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center while playing IVmaj7. Works universally. The G# gives natural Lydian quality.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in A major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: E Mixolydian shares all notes with A major parent scale. The b7 (D) creates dominant 7th character. Perfect for V-I resolution and bluesy dominant sounds.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The D (b7) creates dominant character leading to A. The G# (3rd) is crucial for major sound. Emphasize G# and D for strong V-I pull.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in A major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution. Use for blues, rock, funk, and all dominant situations.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major provides pure dominant triad without 7th. Creates stronger, more classical V-I resolution. The D# (maj7) is brighter than Mixolydian's D natural.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More classical approach to V chord. The D# creates major 7th rather than dominant 7th. Perfect for traditional V-I cadences.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical, traditional, or pure major V chord sound. Perfect for hymns, classical music, and traditional harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes complexity while maintaining strong dominant character. All notes are safe and consonant. Perfect for country and rock styles.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - all notes perfectly consonant. Great for simple, memorable V chord melodies. Perfect for country and folk.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for country, folk, and rock V chords. Ideal for simple, singable melodies over dominant. Essential for accessible styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major scale contains all V chord tones. Thinking in the key center maintains tonal clarity. E Mixolydian is the same as A Ionian starting on E.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize E as root. The D creates natural Mixolydian/dominant character. Maintains key center focus.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic approach to V. Maintains key center awareness. Works universally in all contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E Mixolydian contains all four chord tones perfectly. The b7 (D) is a chord tone, making this the natural choice. Essential for V7-I resolution in all styles.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the 3rd (G#) and b7 (D) - these create dominant character and resolve to A. The tritone (G#-D) wants to resolve outward.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I progressions (Bm7-E7-Amaj7). Use in jazz, rock, blues, pop, and all styles with dominant harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Thinking in A major over E7 maintains key center. E Mixolydian and A Ionian contain the same notes. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think A major, emphasize E as root. The D natural creates dominant 7th automatically. Maintains clear tonal direction to A.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center awareness over V7. Works universally. Great for teaching diatonic harmony concepts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds blue notes (A, Bb) for authentic blues dominant sound. The b5 (Bb) creates characteristic blues tension. Essential for blues and rock.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use the Bb (b5) as passing tone to B. Creates authentic blues sound. The minor 3rd (G) adds bluesy tension over major chord.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for blues and blues-rock E7 chords. Perfect for 12-bar blues. Use when you want authentic, gritty blues dominant sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains b9, #9, #11, b13 for maximum tension. Used in jazz for dramatic V7 resolution. Creates dissonant, modern sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Maximum tension - use for dramatic jazz resolutions. All alterations (#9, b9, #11, b13) create strong pull to A major. Advanced sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for modern jazz E7alt chords. Perfect for dramatic V7-I resolution in bebop, post-bop, and fusion. Very advanced sound.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with A major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: F# minor is the vi of A major. Shares all seven notes with A major, providing natural minor center within the key. The most common minor chord in A major progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes from A major work perfectly. This is the natural minor sound in the key. Perfect for vi-IV-I-V and similar progressions.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in A major. Perfect for I-V-vi-IV, vi-IV-I-V progressions. Works in pop, rock, folk, and all styles. The relative minor relationship is fundamental.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Dorian adds D# (major 6th) for brighter minor sound. More sophisticated than natural minor. Common in jazz and contemporary styles on vi chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The D# (major 6th) creates jazzy, bright minor color. More modern than Aeolian. Perfect for sophisticated vi minor harmony.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, jazzy vi minor sound. Perfect for jazz, funk, R&B, and neo-soul. Brighter alternative to natural minor on vi.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes complexity for guaranteed consonance. Perfect for memorable melodies and simple improvisation. Universal minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - all notes safe. Perfect for singable melodies. Great for pop and rock vi minor approaches.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, memorable vi minor melodies. Ideal for pop, rock, and singer-songwriter styles. Essential for accessible, singable lines.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major and F# minor share all notes. Thinking in A major over F#m maintains key center awareness. F# Aeolian = A Ionian starting on F#.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think A major, emphasize F# as root. Maintains tonal clarity and key center. All notes work perfectly for diatonic vi minor.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for understanding relative major/minor relationship. Works universally. Great for teaching and maintaining key center focus.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The natural minor sound is perfect for vi minor 7 in major keys. Most common choice for vim7 chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. This is the standard sound for vim7. Perfect for emotional, melancholic sections in major keys.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in A major. Perfect for I-V-vim7-IV progressions. Works in pop, rock, folk, and all styles. The definitive vim7 sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The major 6th (D#) creates brighter, more sophisticated minor 7 sound. Common in jazz and R&B for vim7 chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The D# (13th) creates jazz flavor. More sophisticated than natural minor. Perfect for R&B and neo-soul vim7 approaches.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern vim7 sound. Perfect for jazz, R&B, neo-soul, and contemporary pop. Brighter alternative to Aeolian.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple and effective. All A major notes work over F#m7. Maintains key center connection. F# Aeolian contains the same notes as A Ionian.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize F# as root. Maintains tonal clarity. Perfect for understanding diatonic relationships.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic approach to vim7. Maintains key awareness. Works universally in all contexts and styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: G# Locrian is the vii° mode in A major. Contains all diminished chord tones plus the characteristic b2. Creates leading tone function to I.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Highly unstable - resolves strongly to A major. The G# is the leading tone to A. Use for dramatic tension before I chord resolution.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use sparingly for leading tone tension. Creates strong pull to I chord. Common in classical and jazz for dramatic resolutions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The whole-half diminished scale provides tensions for diminished chords. Symmetrical construction. Advanced jazz concept.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very dissonant and tense. Use for maximum tension before resolution. Creates symmetrical, angular melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for advanced jazz vii° approaches. Very dissonant. Perfect for bebop and modern jazz. Creates maximum tension.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Source of vii° in minor keys
scaleCompatibility.rationale: G#dim comes from A harmonic minor. Creates classical minor key resolution. The G# is raised 7th of A minor.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Classical approach to diminished. The G# leads to A in minor contexts. Creates traditional classical resolution.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical or neoclassical approaches. Perfect for minor key contexts. Creates traditional leading tone resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: G# Locrian contains all four chord tones. The vii mode naturally produces m7b5 quality. Essential for understanding diatonic half-diminished chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very unstable - creates strong pull to I major. The G# leads to A. Use for tension before resolution. The b5 and b9 create characteristic dissonance.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for viim7b5 in major keys. Used in jazz ii-V-I (G#m7b5-C#7-F#m) in minor contexts. Creates leading tone tension.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major contains all notes of G#m7b5. Simple diatonic thinking. G# Locrian = A Ionian starting on G#.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think in A major, emphasize G# as root. Creates natural Locrian sound. Maintains key center awareness.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic understanding of viim7b5. Maintains key center connection. Works for teaching and practical application.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of rock, blues, and country music. Three-chord progression that has powered countless hits.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, resolute, and optimistic. Creates clear sense of home (I), departure (IV), tension (V), and resolution back to I.
famousSongs: La Bamba, Twist and Shout, Wild Thing, Louie Louie
commonProgressions.tips: The most fundamental progression in Western music. Perfect for beginners. Can be enhanced with 7th chords (A-D-E7) for more color.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: The modern pop progression, also called the 'Axis of Awesome' progression. Dominates contemporary pop music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile - can sound happy, sad, or nostalgic depending on melody and rhythm. Very singable.
famousSongs: Let It Be, No Woman No Cry, With or Without You, Don't Stop Believin'
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely versatile. Works for verses, choruses, or entire songs. Can be played in any order (vi-IV-I-V is also common). The vi minor chord adds emotional depth.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The classic '50s progression, also called the doo-wop changes. Foundational to early rock and roll.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Nostalgic, romantic, and optimistic. The vi minor creates brief melancholy before resolving through IV-V-I.
famousSongs: Stand By Me, Every Breath You Take, Blue Moon, In the Still of the Night
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro vibes. The vi chord adds emotional color. Can be enhanced with 7th chords (Amaj7-F#m7-Dmaj7-E7) for jazz flavor.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression known as the 'rhythm changes.' Essential in jazz repertoire.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Creates strong harmonic motion through circle-of-fifths root movement (vi-ii-V-I).
famousSongs: I Got Rhythm, Oleo, Anthropology, Rhythm-a-ning
commonProgressions.tips: Essential for jazz. The ii-V-I at the end creates strong resolution. Practice with 7th chords. Can be enhanced with tensions (9ths, 11ths, 13ths).

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: The fundamental jazz progression. The building block of jazz harmony and the strongest resolution in tonal music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Creates powerful sense of resolution. Smooth voice leading. The gold standard for functional harmony.
famousSongs: Autumn Leaves, Satin Doll, Fly Me to the Moon, All the Things You Are
commonProgressions.tips: Master this progression to unlock jazz. Practice with extensions: Bm9-E13-Amaj9. The V7 can be altered (E7alt) for more tension. Essential for jazz improvisation.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic pop/folk progression with smooth voice leading. Very common in singer-songwriter music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Warm, hopeful, and melodically smooth. Creates gentle emotional arc from bright to contemplative and back.
famousSongs: Hey Soul Sister, Ho Hey, Home, Count on Me
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for acoustic guitar. Can add 7ths (Amaj7-Dmaj7-F#m7-E7) for sophistication. The vi minor adds depth without sadness.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop/rock progression starting on the minor vi. Creates emotional depth immediately.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution. Perfect for verses that grow in emotional intensity.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact. Great for verses leading to brighter choruses. Can loop indefinitely. The I chord feels like a moment of relief.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Ascending progression through scale degrees. Less common but emotionally effective.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Builds tension through ascending harmony. The iii minor adds sophistication. Creates sense of climbing or building.
famousSongs: Kiss From a Rose, Wouldn't It Be Nice sections
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord is underused but effective. Creates smooth voice leading. Great for building sections or bridges. Can add 7ths for more color.

commonProgressions.name: I-II-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Blues-rock progression using the major II chord (borrowed from Mixolydian mode).
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Powerful, bluesy, and modal. The II major creates strong upward motion. Very common in blues-rock.
famousSongs: Good Riddance (Time of Your Life), Sympathy for the Devil sections
commonProgressions.tips: The B major is borrowed from A Mixolydian. Creates modal sound. Great for vamps and rock anthems. Use power chords for heavier sound.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression combining relative minor with ii-V-I. Creates smooth descending motion.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution. Perfect for jazz ballads and extended harmonic passages.
famousSongs: Common in jazz standards and ballads
commonProgressions.tips: Extends the ii-V-I with a vi start. Practice with jazz voicings. Can substitute chords (F#m7b5, E7alt). Great for learning jazz harmony.

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal rock progression borrowing the bVII from Mixolydian. Creates strong, anthemic sound.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold, anthemic, and powerful. The G major creates modal flavor. Very popular in 70s-90s rock.
famousSongs: Sweet Child O' Mine, Summer of '69 sections, Wonderwall sections
commonProgressions.tips: The G major is borrowed from A Mixolydian. Creates powerful rock sound. Perfect for arena rock and anthems. Use power chords for maximum impact.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: High-energy rock progression with emphasis on dominant function. Creates driving momentum.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Creates strong forward momentum. The double V emphasizes tension and release.
famousSongs: Common in uptempo rock songs and power pop
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. The repeated V creates urgency. Perfect for choruses and climactic moments. Keep the rhythm driving.
